channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: MotionRec for PS5, Switch launches April 2
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Switch

Puzzle action game currently available for PC.

The PlayStation 5 and Switch versions of puzzle action game MotionRec will launch on April 2, publisher PLAYISM and developer HANDSUM announced.

MotionRec first launched for PC via Steam on October 27, 2025.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

MotionRec is a recording puzzle action game where you progress through stages by recording and playing back your own movements.

Rec, The Civilization-Recording Robot

MotionRec is a recording-based puzzle action game built around the concept of recording and playback. Use this ability to guide Rec, the civilization-recording robot, through a devastated world.

The Power of MotionRec

You can record the trajectory of your movements, from walking to jumping. Use stage gimmicks to create and record more complex movements to traverse the world.

Record your movements…

…and play them back in another location!

This will let you reach places too high to jump up to, or even access spots where there’s nothing to stand on. You can only play back your movements a certain number of times midair, however, and this is refreshed every time you land on the ground.

Make good use of the MotionRec ability to unravel the mysteries behind this devastated world ruled by machines!

Staff

  • Studio: HANDSUM inc.
  • Pixel Art: m7kenji
  • Music: Kyohei Fujita
  • Developer: shoma

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer

English

Japanese

Simplified Chinese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/motionrec-for-ps5-switch-launches-april-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Laid-Back Camp: Camping Cook-Off! launches March 18 for PC, later for Switch and mobile
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Smartphone
  • Switch

Japanese-only at launch. English language support coming at a later date.

“Camping action game” Laid-Back Camp: Camping Cook-Off! will launch for PC via Steam on March 18 for 3,480 yen, followed by Switch and mobile at a later date, enish announced. The game will only support Japanese at launch, but will gradually add support for additional languages, including English and Traditional Chinese.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Laid-Back Camp: Camping Cook-Off! is a cooperative camping action game for one-to-four players, set in the world of the popular TV anime Laid-Back Camp.

Players can take control of familiar characters such as Nadeshiko and Rin, and tackle a variety of camping tasks including cooking, setting up tents, and preparing campfires. Each stage offers unique gimmicks and challenges, requiring you to work efficiently within a time limit while enjoying the excitement of cooperating with friends.

Key Features

  • One-to-Four-Player Support – Experience the satisfaction of careful planning in solo play, or the fun chaos of cooperation in multiplayer.
  • Story Mode – In addition to original game episodes, you can also experience memorable scenes from the anime.
  • Varied Camping Actions – Cook meals, pitch tents, prepare campfires, and enjoy an authentic outdoor experience.
  • Scoring System – Get evaluated based on time, steps, and teamwork. Challenge yourself repeatedly to sharpen your skills.
  • Planned Downloadable Content – Additional stages, outfits, and more long-term content are on the way.

Cast

The main characters are voiced by the same talented cast from the anime, bringing the atmosphere of the series directly into the game.

Blending the laid-back and heartwarming camping experience of Laid-Back Camp with chaotic cooperative action, this is a new style of multiplayer fun. Recommended not only for anime fans but also for anyone looking for a lively and engaging multiplayer game.

Spend the ultimate camping time with your friends and family!



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/laid-back-camp-camping-cook-off-launches-march-18-for-pc-later-for-switch-and-mobile
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales details Age of Reconstruction
item.content:encoded:
Get the details below.

■ Story

A Story of Four Ages across a Thousand Years

To lift the curse on Princess Heuria of Huther, Elliot and Faie will cross through the Doorway of Time to visit different periods in history.

They will travel from their own time, the Age of Safekeeping, further and further into the past. Across every age, a terrible threat looms over Elliot and Faie’s adventure.

—Age of Safekeeping: The Present

—Age of Reconstruction: The Dark Ages

—Age of Magic: A Golden Age

—Age of Budding: The Birth of Civilization

■ Age of Reconstruction

Further into the past from Elliot’s home Age of Safekeeping is the Age of Reconstruction. Amidst the ruins of a more prosperous time, humanity’s remnants live on the brink of extinction.

There is no spell of Safekeeping in this age to repel the beastmen. Humans cling together in a village called Littlehope, eking out an existence with meager resources and defenses. Though most of the villagers have given in to despair, one young man with a glimmer of hope in his heart looks to the future.

■ Characters

Heurich (voiced by Daniel Sean Smith in English, Nobuhiko Okamoto in Japanese)

An adventurer from the village of Littlehope. He finds a kindred spirit in Elliot after their chance encounter, and shares with our hero information about the ruins and treasures discovered during his own adventures. While traveling the continent in search of a cure for his childhood friend Diona’s illness, Heurich hopes the power and wisdom gained on his journeys will be the key to pulling the world out of its dark ages.

Diona (voiced by Katrina Salisbury in English, Haruka Tomatsu in Japanese)

A young woman who grew up alongside Heurich in Littlehope. A chronic illness keeps her indoors most days. Her caring nature has earned her a warm place in many a heart of the other villagers. She constantly frets about Heurich’s reckless adventuring, so much so that their conversations often lead to quarrels.

Heurich (voiced by Rebecca Wang in English, Chiwa Saito in Japanese)

Littlehope’s resident historian and mutual friend of Heurich and Diona. Despite the world’s desperate condition, her passion for learning about humanity’s past drives her research. She takes an interest in Elliot and his similarities to Heurich, and offers him her expert advice.

■ System: Accessories

In addition to weapons and magicite, Elliot can equip and change accessories at any time from the equipment menu. Accessories are imbued with various effects beneficial to adventuring and should be selected in accordance with the demands of the ruins or foes Elliot currently faces.

—Equip the discerning monocle to double the window acceptable for a precise guard. It’s the prefect accessory for countering enemies that attack from a distance or bosses with an overbearing offense.

—The defalling charm protects Elliot from damage taken when falling into pits and lava—welcome insurance to have when navigating narrow footing and floating platforms.

■ System: Quests

In each age are people who need Elliot’s help. Hear them out and undertake the quests they give. There are some items that can only be obtained by fulfilling their requests.

—Begin a quest by speaking to those with a quest icon above their heads. Quests may involve anything from gathering items to fighting beastmen and more.

—Story progress may render some quests unable to be completed. When such an alert is displayed, make it a point to clear those quests before progressing further.

■ System: Shrines of Life

Mysterious structures known as shrines of life dot the map. Inside of them, players’ skills and intellect are tested, with special rewards awaiting those capable of overcoming the trials.

—A shrine of life always has an adventuring guidepost at its entrance, so you can quickly try again when things don’t go as planned.

—Trials include both combat-related and puzzle-solving challenges. Clearing a trial awards a shrine of life, which restores Elliot’s vitality. Collect four shards of life to increase Elliot’s max vitality by one.

■ System: Bonus Drops

Defeating enemies in succession without taking damage will trigger a bonus in which the defeated drop more loot. Taking damage will terminate the bonus.

—Bonus time is your chance to rake in the tul! Trounce as many foes as you can to fund your further adventures.

—In addition to tul, bonus drops increase the likelihood of finding arrows, bombs, and items that raise attack and defense. Continuing to defeat enemies during the bonus will raise the bonus level and the item drop rate in kind.

■ Adventuring Diversion: Cat Collecting

No matter the era, Philabieldia is filled with cats. If Elliot finds one, he can take it under his wing to bring it to a cat-fancying traveler in town who will reward Elliot for the number of cats he’s collected. Afterward, Elliot can care for and play with any cats he’s brought back.

—If you hear a cat’s meow during your adventuring, be sure to take a look around. Pay attention to any help Faie may have to offer, too.

—Rewards include all sorts of helpful items, such as those to help find more cats or increase the number of arrows and bombs Elliot can carry. Don’t take a catnap on cat collecting!

—You can feed cats you’ve brought back to town, from a selection of fish, meat, and cheese. Different cats have different tastes, and filling them up on the flavors they prefer will make them like you more.

—Once a cat has sufficiently warmed up to you, you can invite it to the orphanage to play with cat toys. Visit the item shop for all your pet care needs.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/the-adventures-of-elliot-the-millennium-tales-details-age-of-reconstruction
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: MONGIL: STAR DIVE launches April 15 for PC, iOS, and Android, later for PS5 and Xbox Series
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Smartphone
  • Xbox Series

New gameplay trailer and developer commentary released.

Action RPG MONGIL: STAR DIVE will launch for PC via the official launcher and Epic Games Store, iOS, and Android on April 15, followed by PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series at a later date, publisher Netmarble and developer Netmarble Monster announced. Pre-registration is available now via App Store, Google Play, and the official website.

“We are focusing all of our efforts on final polishing to deliver MONGIL: STAR DIVE at the highest level of quality,” said Netmarble Monster CEO Ken Kim in a press release. “We will repay the anticipation of players around the world with a game that meets their expectations.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Netmarble:

>MONGIL: STAR DIVE is the long-awaited sequel to the beloved 2013 mobile collectible RPG Monster Taming. Built with Unreal Engine 5, the title delivers high-quality cinematic storytelling, dynamic real-time combat, and a three-character tag battle system that emphasizes fast-paced action and strategic team switching.

The game features a cast of charming and distinctive characters alongside its signature “Monsterling Collection” system, which allows players to capture, collect, and synthesize monsters as companions. These Monsterlings can be equipped and combined to enhance combat performance and deepen progression strategy.

Ahead of launch, MONGIL: STAR DIVE has undergone multiple global closed beta tests to refine gameplay quality and stability. The game has also been showcased at major international gaming events including Gamescom and Tokyo Game Show, where players and media experienced hands-on demonstrations and live presentations.

At G-STAR 2025, the team drew attention by unveiling a new Korean-inspired region and characters for the first time. During GDC 2026, which is taking place in San Francisco this week, Netmarble has attracted global media attention through a collaborative on-site demo experience with Xbox and the release of new playthrough footage.

Watch a new gameplay trailer and developer presentation below.

Gameplay Trailer

English

Japanese

Korean

Release Date Reveal: Developer Commentary

English

Japanese

Korean



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/mongil-star-dive-launches-april-15-for-pc-ios-and-android-later-for-ps5-and-xbox-series
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Powerful Pro Baseball 2026-2027 opening movie
item.content:encoded:
• PS4
  • Switch

Featuring the theme song "Highlight" sung by Anko.

Konami has released the opening movie for Powerful Pro Baseball 2026-2027, featuring the theme song “Highlight” sung by Anko.

Powerful Pro Baseball 2026-2027 is due out for PlayStation 4 and Switch on June 11 in Japan. Read more about the game here.

Watch the opening movie below.

Opening Movie



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/powerful-pro-baseball-2026-2027-opening-movie
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Cozy diving adventure game Splash Divers announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

"Cozy Dive" playtest sign-ups now available.

Publisher Spiral Up Games and developer TinyMo GAMES have announced cozy diving adventure game Splash Divers for PC (Steam). “Cozy Dive” playtest sign-ups are available now.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Immerse yourself in an unforgettable diving vacation! Break free from the limits of air supply and dive into the beauty of the ocean and its incredible marine life. Build your own personalized ocean encyclopedia, connect with fellow divers worldwide, and savor these moments of leisure.

Key Features

  • Dive Into Ocean Bliss – Wave goodbye to countdowns and health bars as you dive headfirst into the ocean with a splash! Immerse yourself at your own pace, bask in the play of sunlight through the water, and indulge yourself in the enchanting undersea realm. Explore freely, befriend sea turtles, track whales, and interact with lively schools of vibrant fish.
  • Snap Quirky Sea Neighbors – The ocean is a bustling hub of intriguing creatures. Press the shutter and seize the moments! Witness lobsters darting in reverse and crabs parading sideways… but beware the inky wrath of squids if you get too close! Encounter these marvelous neighbors, snap memorable scenes, expand your oceanic encyclopedia, and turn each dive into a trove of excitement.
  • Gather Shells, Log Your Dives – In addition to photographing marine life, dive deeper to collect shells and conches hidden beneath the waves. Craft your unique dive journal using stickers gifted by locals. Record your enchanting undersea memories with beautiful shells, exquisite stickers, and vivid imagery.
  • Share the Wonders of the Deep – Connect with your fellow divers! Coming together from all corners of the world, you unite in a shared passion for the sea. Dive together, exchange collections, join in bonfire celebrations, and uncover each other’s stories… In Splash Divers, your sole aim is to unwind completely and savor the laid-back island moments.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/cozy-diving-adventure-game-splash-divers-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Next mainline Neptunia game to be announced on March 19
item.content:encoded:
Over 10 years after the last mainline game.

Compile Heart will announce the long-awaited next mainline entry in the Neptunia series on March 19 at 5:00 a.m. PT / 8:00 a.m. ET / 21:00 JST, the company teased. You will be able to watch it on YouTube.

The new game would be the first mainline game in the series in over 10 years, following Hyperdimension Neptunia (2010), Hyperdimension Neptunia mk2 (2011), Hyperdimension Neptunia Victory (2012), and Megadimension Neptunia VII (2015).

Watch a teaser trailer below. Visit the teaser website here.

Next Mainline Neptunia Game Teaser Trailer

Update 03/12/26 at 2:25 a.m.: The first public hands-on demo for the unannounced Neptunia game will be playable at Happinet Game Fes! 2026 at Bellesalle Akihabara from March 28 to 29, Happinet announced.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/next-mainline-neptunia-game-to-be-announced-on-march-19
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Curse Warrior second trailer, latest details and screenshots
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Nippon Ichi Software and developer guruguru have released a new trailer, information, and screenshots for dungeon crawler RPG Curse Warrior. As announced earlier today, a demo is also now available for all platforms in Japan.

■ The Reality of Character Loss

When allies are defeated by enemies, they are not knocked out—they are “lost” on the spot. Lost allies will never return, nor can they be revived or resurrected.

Even allies you have spent a long time training can be lost with a single bad decision.

These are not unavoidable “accidents”—they are the results of your decisions.

Every decision you make in a dungeon carries the risk of losing your allies. Dungeons are randomly generated, so no two are the same, and their difficulty constantly changes. Danger is always close at hand.

—Allies killed by attacks are “lost” and will never return.

■ Irrational “Curses”

Curses that infest the dungeon will mercilessly bare their fangs at the player.

Your actions may be restricted. Your abilities may be stripped away. Your allies may be weakened.

The full effects of a curse are only understood once you experience it firsthand.

To survive, you must accept the curses’ irrationality and make use of them.

However, some curses can have beneficial effects, such as restoring health, dealing additional damage, or allowing you to deflect attacks with a parry. Survival depends on your ability to skillfully control curses.

—Curses with harmful effects can slowly drive adventurers to their deaths.

■ Strategy: “Who to Sacrifice?”

Jobs, party composition, items… players have a wide range of choices.

Who should stand on the front line? Who should stay in the back line? And sometimes, who should be abandoned? In Curse Warrior, strategy requires a willingness to make sacrifices.

There is not always a correct choice. Each job has strengths and weaknesses on the front and back lines, making their placement an important strategic decision.

—Clerics and Paladins, with high HP and durability, can take monster attacks from the front line.

■ Items

Before entering a dungeon, you can purchase items at the shop. Early on, you will have limited funds, so choosing what and how much to bring is key to survival.

—Provisions can be brought without spending funds, so make sure to bring them.

■ Demo Now Available

—All you carry over is knowledge.

A demo that lets players experience the irrationality and tension of Curse Warrior is now available.

All allies you train, equipment you acquire, and levels you gain will be lost.

All you carry over to the full game is knowledge of “how one dies,” “what will be lost if I act,” and “how one survives.”

And that knowledge is the most valuable resource you can have..

■ Few Clues Exist in This Irrational World

Those who enter unprepared will surely lose it all. Only the few who understand the world will survive.

Curse Warrior features systems that serve as the “keys to survival.”

■ The Role of Jobs

One who blocks enemy attacks on the front line. One whose skills pair well with curses. One who specializes in support, increasing allies’ chances of survival.

Each job has clear strengths and weaknesses, and none are all-rounders. Who stands in front, who is protected, and who is cast aside? Understanding jobs directly affects survival.

There are eight adventurer jobs, each with their own preferred attack types, curse resistances, and optimal party positions. Adventurers also possess two skills. Keep these characteristics in mind when forming your party.

At the “Wolf Moon Tavern,” you can hire a variety of adventurers, whose abilities differ depending on their job.

Hiring an adventurer with strong abilities or useful skills can be key to clearing a dungeon. Over time, new adventurers will appear at the tavern and become available for hire.

—Each adventurer has different abilities and skills. New adventurers visit the tavern over time.

■ Purification Facility: Rainbow Eyes

Rainbow Eyes is a purification facility that purifies curses.

By purifying curses, you can remove unfavorable curses while keeping beneficial ones.

Upgrading the facility increases the number of adventurers and curses that can be purified at once.

—Curses that disadvantage adventurers can be removed through purification.

■ Exploration Items: “Lifelines”

While exploring caves, exploration items such as potions, antidotes, and hammers are essential.

Use them, or save them?

That single decision could determine who survives and who is lost.

Dungeons offer a variety of items.

Some of these items strengthen adventurers when equipped.

Finding and equipping these items is essential to making adventurers stronger.

—Equipment can also be obtained as quest rewards or after battles.

■ Curse Fusion

Curses have affinities, and depending on their combination, they can become significantly stronger. This is called “Curse Fusion.”

Combining two or three curses creates a more powerful curse, transforming it into a higher-level curse with an increased activation rate.

You can carry up to six curses. If a curse has two effects, up to 12 different effects can activate. The timing and probability of each effect can cause the cursed character’s stats to fluctuate in complex ways.

Curses can have irreversible consequences. Only those who understand, accept, and are prepared to master them can take this gamble.

—Two curses can combine to form a new, higher-level curse.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/curse-warrior-second-trailer-latest-details-and-screenshots
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Animula Nook PC alpha test set for March 19
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Sign-ups available until March 15.

The alpha test for “lilliputian world” fantasy life simulation game Animula Nook for PC (Steam) will begin on March 19, publisher Tencent Games and developer LilliLandia Games announced. Sign-ups are available via the official website until March 15.

Animula Nook will be available for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, PC via Steam and Epic Games Store, and Mac. A release date has yet to be announced.

Watch a new trailer below.

Alpha Test Trailer

English

Japanese

Korean



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/animula-nook-pc-alpha-test-set-for-march-19
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Bunny Garden 2 launches April 16
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Switch

Dating simulation adventure game sequel dated.

Dating simulation adventure game sequel Bunny Garden 2 will launch for Switch and PC via Steam on April 16 for $24.99, developer qureate announced. New characters Kuon and Runa were also introduced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its official website:

About

Another unforgettable night is about to begin…

Bunny Garden‘s brand-new title finally arrives!

This latest installment in the romance adventure game series is designed for gentlemen of pure heart who frequent the gentlemen’s retreat “Bunny Garden” and connect with its uniquely charming cast.

Reunite with familiar faces while welcoming new additions, delivering an even more immersive experience than its predecessor.

The “gentleman’s games” have evolved further; how you enjoy them is entirely up to you.

Forget the hardships of daily life and indulge in sweet, heart-pounding nights.

Story

Haito Kanda (name changeable) lost his job due to a major mistake at work, and had fallen into despair over everything.

Just as he was heading to his usual cheap pub to drown his sorrows…

A woman suddenly spoke to him, noticing his dejected expression.

That was the fateful encounter…

Guided by her, he stepped into a sanctuary for gentlemen, where women as adorable as rabbits welcomed him…

“Bunny Garden.”

What changes will this encounter bring to their lives, and yours?

It’s all up to you…

A new Bunny Garden, set in a different world line from the previous title.

A brand-new story and a cast you have yet to meet await you.

System

  • With a Drink in Hand, Enjoy Sweet and Delightful Moments With the Cast
    • Bunny Garden is a social gathering place brimming with relaxation and excitement for gentlemen. Savor an extensive selection of drinks and snacks while enjoying conversation with the uniquely charming cast.
    • Ordering menu items tailored to a cast member’s preferences might just boost your likability?
    • Furthermore, as the drinks flow and they become tipsy, they might reveal sides of themselves you’ve never seen before.
  • Allow Your Favorite Girl’s Song to Intoxicate Your Heart
    • Each cast member has their own signature song. You might find yourself captivated by that lively girl’s energetic performance!
    • Plus, when they sing while tipsy, you might just catch a glimpse of a surprising side you’ve never seen before…
    • Laugh, feel your heart flutter, and get a little excited. Come see the cast’s stage performances with your own eyes.

Characters

  • Kana (voiced by Mio Hoshitani) – With her carefree smile, she treats everyone equally. She is like Bunny Garden’s own sun. Her brightness and kindness unwittingly melt customers’ hearts. Yet behind that smile lies a strong resolve to pursue a certain dream.
  • Rin (voiced by Eri Suzuki) – The life of the party at Bunny Garden. Free-spirited and friendly, she talks casually with first-time customers to close the distance. Sometimes she teases like a little devil, enjoying the reactions—leaving many people flustered. But behind that free-spiritedness lies a slightly unexpected side…
  • Miuka (voiced by Masumi Tazawa) – The sharp-tongued one at Bunny Garden, who speaks her mind without hesitation. Countless customers have been stung by her piercing words. Yet there’s no malice whatsoever. Rather, her frankness and unwavering stance possess a strange charm that somehow becomes addictive.
  • Erisa (voiced by Yukina Shuto) – A cast member on her trial period. She tends to act aloof, but is actually a very kind and considerate girl with a tsundere personality. She has a severe tendency to overthink things, where even the protagonist’s casual words or gestures can instantly send her imagination running wild in that direction. She may seem strong-willed, but she actually loves imagining romantic scenarios and sometimes gets lost in her own fantasy world.
  • Kuon (voiced by Ruriko Noguchi) – Her words and actions are utterly indecipherable at first meeting—mysterious and bewitching, leaving you unsure if she’s joking or serious. One moment she’ll suddenly close the distance, the next she’ll drift away, leaving behind cryptic words. It’s as if she’s testing you. That very elusiveness is her charm.
  • Runa (voiced by Hina Nastume) – Her distinctive tone and slightly cheeky attitude make her stand out. She carries an enigmatic aura, using phrasing that seems to test people while intently observing their expressions and reactions. At unexpected moments, she flashes a knowing smile, as if she alone holds some secret, suggesting something deeper lies beneath the surface—something yet to be revealed.

Staff

  • Character Design: Waon Inui
  • Scenario: Takayoshi Muto (Synthese)
  • Producer: Yujiro Usuda

View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/bunny-garden-2-launches-april-16
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pokemon Pokopia sales top 2.2 million in four days
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
